claim_text: Explicit breaking vs no_clear_breaking classification is sufficient for prioritization
counterargument: No_clear_breaking can hide breaking changes in sparse or untranslated release notes
boundary_condition_not_hold: Repos with weak changelog or non-English release notes
recommendation_for_c: Add changelog-quality and coverage flag to matrix

claim_text: Pin to v0.10.x / v0.145.x is adequate rollback suggestion
counterargument: Pin alone does not define how long the pin is safe (CVE, support EOL)
boundary_condition_not_hold: Long-lived pin with security or support window
recommendation_for_c: Add pin expiry and security-backport policy

claim_text: Upgrade order MCP first then serving then orchestration is universally correct
counterargument: Order can be wrong when critical CVE or compliance forces serving or orchestration first
recommendation_for_c: Add event-driven reorder and exception path

claim_text: Parsing failure rate increase >0.5% blocks rollout
counterargument: Single threshold can be too strict or too loose across different schema surface areas
boundary_condition_not_hold: Heterogeneous telemetry or multi-tenant schema mix
recommendation_for_c: Tier threshold by schema criticality and tenant count

claim_text: Policy deny drift >2% on approved traffic blocks rollout
counterargument: Golden set may not cover new policy dimensions introduced in upgrade
boundary_condition_not_hold: Policy engine adds new condition types
recommendation_for_c: Require golden set refresh and coverage check before gate

claim_text: p95 regress >15% or error rate >1.5x baseline blocks rollout
counterargument: Baseline may be stale or unrepresentative of canary traffic mix
recommendation_for_c: Define baseline refresh rule and canary traffic representativeness

claim_text: Versioned adapters for PyRIT/TruLens/OTel absorb API drift
counterargument: Adapters add maintenance burden and can lag behind multi-version support
recommendation_for_c: Document adapter maintenance SLA and version support window

claim_text: Separate policy bundle rollout from runtime binary rollout is always feasible
counterargument: Some policy engines are embedded or single-binary; separation not possible
recommendation_for_c: Allow combined rollout with stricter canary when separation not possible

claim_text: High/Medium/Low migration complexity is stable per repo
counterargument: Complexity can change with new major version or ecosystem dependency shift
boundary_condition_not_hold: Major version or transitive dependency break
recommendation_for_c: Re-evaluate complexity on major or dependency-boundary change

claim_text: Global failure type frequency from 40 repos is representative
counterargument: Sample is biased toward eval/observability repos; agent/runtime may differ
recommendation_for_c: Stratify failure stats by layer or publish coverage disclaimer

claim_text: Rollback on error rate >2x baseline for 10 minutes is sufficient
counterargument: Ten-minute window can be too long for user-facing or revenue-critical paths
recommendation_for_c: Tier rollback window by blast radius and business criticality

counterargument: Classification is based on release notes only; code or config break may be undocumented
boundary_condition_not_hold: Repos with sparse or automated changelog
recommendation_for_c: Add code-diff or compatibility-test requirement for no_clear_breaking

claim_text: Recommendation A (LangGraph + MCP + Qdrant + LiteLLM + Istio + Langfuse) is production-default
counterargument: Single combo assumes org has mesh and multi-stack capacity
recommendation_for_c: Label as scale profile and add minimal profile

claim_text: P0 wave in Week 1-2 and P1 in Week 3-5 is execution-ready
counterargument: Wave timing assumes dedicated upgrade owner and no parallel incidents
recommendation_for_c: Add dependency on owner availability and incident freeze

claim_text: Keep previous known-good artifact bundle is sufficient rollback prep
counterargument: Bundle may not include state or config generated at runtime
recommendation_for_c: Define state snapshot and config export for stateful components

claim_text: temporary-exception-policy with expiry and compensating controls is sufficient
counterargument: Expiry without enforcement leads to permanent exceptions
recommendation_for_c: Add automated exception expiry check and escalation

claim_text: Two-step rollout canary then partial prod then full prod is recommended
counterargument: Two-step may be insufficient when blast radius is cross-region or cross-tenant
recommendation_for_c: Add step count and scope by blast-radius class

claim_text: docs_example_drift and dependency_version_conflict are top failure types
counterargument: Frequency is from issue/discussion text; may not match production incident root cause
boundary_condition_not_hold: Production incident taxonomy not aligned with repo issues
recommendation_for_c: Cross-check with internal incident taxonomy and add production-weight option
